mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month payroll employment growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor demand and consumer income expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days through increased corporate earnings expectations and reduced recession risk.
evidence_refs: fact:October 2015 payroll level 142,654k indicates sustained hiring momentum
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of months with payroll growth > 150k.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains below 0.25% and inflation (CPIAUCSL) shows no acceleration above 2.5% year-over-year, the zero-lower-bound monetary regime persists, suppressing discount rates and supporting equity valuations with positive 60-day forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:October 2015 FEDFUNDS at 0.12%, CPIAUCSL at 237.566 index
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 45% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window during ZLB regime.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales (RSAFS) exceeding 1.5% signals weakening consumer demand and reduced near-term corporate revenue growth, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as forward earnings estimates are revised downward.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth decelerates below 0.5% month-over-month while unemployment (UNRATE) rises simultaneously, signaling supply-side weakness and labor market deterioration that reduces profit margins and equity returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO MoM growth < 0.5% AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in 1 month
evidence_refs: fact:September 2015 INDPRO at 107.0917, October UNRATE at 5.0%
regime_hint: late-cycle slowdown with labor market softening
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in > 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.025 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) accelerate above 1,200 thousand units annualized, indicating strong residential investment demand and forward-looking builder confidence that drives positive multiplier effects through construction employment and materials demand, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:September 2015 HOUST at 1,206 thousand units, near trigger threshold
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in fewer than 58% of months with HOUST > 1200k, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 5.2% while payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates below 100k month-over-month, signaling labor market deterioration that increases recession probability and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.025 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates below 2.0% annualized while the federal funds rate remains above 0.5%, signaling monetary policy tightening into a slowing economy that compresses equity risk premiums and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Q2 2015 GDPC1 at 16,394.2 billion chained 2017 dollars, October FEDFUNDS at 0.12%
regime_hint: monetary tightening into weak growth regime
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in > 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) exhibits a 3-month cumulative decline exceeding 1.5% while consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains below 2.0% year-over-year, signaling deflationary supply shock and demand weakness that reduces equity valuations with negative 20-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month cumulative change < -1.5% AND CPIAUCSL YoY growth < 2.0%
regime_hint: deflationary supply shock with demand deterioration
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.025 over any 2-year rolling window.
